text: The patient is positioned supine with reverse Trendelenburg and left tilt. The robotic setup is visible with port sites placed as shown. The surgeon identifies anatomical structures, followed by extended cauterization of the duodenum, including the transverse third portion and pancreatic head, to expose the diverticulum.

The surgeon has fully mobilized the duodenum, revealing the inferior vena cava and left renal vein. The duodenal diverticulum is identified and dissected, exposing its neck between separated muscle layers. The diverticulum is then transected at the neck using a laparoscopic linear stapler or cautery, followed by primary closure.
The surgeon approximates the duodenal wall defect over the diverticular stump, completing the repair. The specimen is placed into an endo bag and retrieved through the assistant port.
